step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the initial assessment. Glycopyrrolate has a unique chemical structure which makes it less likely to penetrate the blood-brain barrier compared to other anticholinergics listed. Additionally, this property also reduces its potential for crossing into the fetal environment via the placenta, making it safer during pregnancy and lactation periods.
